attributes.action: Remove financial burden of UK taxpayers for foreign nationals within the UK.
attributes.additional_details: Successive governments have charged the UK taxpayer for the keeping of foreign nationals who've arrived both legal and illegal. These charges have been levied by both government and the governments decision to absorb foreign actors policies into British law which once again the public has had no say so in. The cost of these policies is unbearable by the public and especially so in a time of rising debt and cost of living. The public would prefer to not pay these charges for foreign nationals.
attributes.background: As the UK taxpayer has never been given a choice about their taxes being used to fund foreign nationals within the UK the government should cease to charge the UK taxpayer as their is no quid pro quo and no mandate I'm aware of for this precedence.

rejection.details: We are not clear what action you are seeking from the UK Government or Parliament.
